content:
# Heavy Metals Pollution

**Summary**: The study of pollutants caused by the presence of heavy metals in soil and water.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-adedapo|Adedapo, 2014]] investigated heavy metal pollution to assess environmental risk.

- [[2015-jawad|Jawad, 2015]] investigated heavy-metals-pollution within the sediment of a coastal wetland.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] addressed heavy-metals-pollution specifically regarding the toxicity of mercury (II).

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] addressed heavy metals pollution, specifically cadmium, in water sources.

- [[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019]] investigated the removal of cadmium from contaminated water using GPHM.

- [[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019]] assessed the accumulation of heavy metals (Cr, Cu, Ni, Hg, Zn) in mangrove tissues.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2014-adedapo|Adedapo, 2014]] ['spatial distribution', 'hot spot identification', 'soil health assessment']

- [[2015-jawad|Jawad, 2015]] investigated heavy-metals-pollution within the sediment of a coastal wetland.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] addressed heavy-metals-pollution specifically regarding the toxicity of mercury (II).

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] addressed heavy metals pollution, specifically cadmium, in water sources.

- [[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019]] investigated the removal of cadmium from contaminated water using GPHM.

- [[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019]] assessed the accumulation of heavy metals (Cr, Cu, Ni, Hg, Zn) in mangrove tissues.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2014-adedapo|Adedapo, 2014]] ['ICP-OES', 'GIS']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Identified that industrial and residential activities contribute significantly to elevated trace metal levels. ([[2014-adedapo|Adedapo, 2014, p. 13]])

- Trace metals are highly persistent and pose risks to human health through bioaccumulation in the food chain. ([[2015-jawad|Jawad, 2015, p. 19]])

- Mercury is highly persistent and can bioaccumulate in the food web, posing significant risks to human health. ([[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016, p. 38]])

- Cadmium is a significant threat to human health and the environment due/to its lack of biodegradability. ([[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017, p. 19]])

- GPHM removed more than 60% of 0.5 ppm cadmium within 10 min. ([[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019, p. 15]])

- Copper concentrations reached up to 70.0 mg/kg in Gurmah Island bark samples. ([[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019, p. 92]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2014-adedapo|Adedapo, 2014]] identifying hot spots and determining if levels exceed safety limits
